summary: We are in a high-stakes sprint to launch the China Going-Global report and Intelligent China webpage while resolving email access issues and finalizing agency selection for social media advertising.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint CKGSB-IE report 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', targeting a digital release by early September for Singapore events at Tech in Asia and Tyson; Felix is meeting with both partners this week to confirm session themes and distribution, while the team begins translating the Chinese report and drafting a one-pager proposal. Concurrently, the Intelligent China webpage must launch this week, and the Asia Star program is already live, with SEO efforts focused on ranking for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing—prioritizing these over US partnerships. Urgent this week: score overseas social media advertising suppliers by July 23 (deadline tomorrow), publish the 'Disruptive Timing' article on the English website, and create the Indonesia Emerging Markets program webpage (Joseph needs program details from Ben). The knowledge website redesign is on hold with a decision to keep the current look but improve menu categorization; Joseph and Julie need to mark feedback on Joker's design and share HTML files. Finally, resolve the Outlook email lockout issue by deleting accounts on all devices and updating passwords—contact JSB backend for unlock confirmation.

meetings.title: 邮箱账号与设备问题排查
meetings.summary: 讨论邮箱账号因旧密码导致锁定问题，建议删除账号重新登录并更新密码，同时检查所有设备上的Outlook配置。

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: The team discussed worship activities and a location visit.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's knowledge website redesign, noting it's similar to current site with improved menu categorization. Discussed incorporating ideas from Joseph's more editorial-style design to show more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, school brochure updates, CES quotes, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, social media agency scouting, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and agency selection.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore in September.

decisions.text: 所有设备需更新密码并重新登录Outlook。
decisions.meetingTitle: 邮箱账号与设备问题排查

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video category; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Keep current knowledge website look and feel, but improve menu categorization and consider adding more articles and indices to homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; Asia Star page already live.
Shun Tian Shu press release will be either a 10-point article or incorporate 10 points into current release, not two separate pieces.
Media Minds is likely the best agency choice given budget constraints and time sensitivity.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools' leadership and possibly an industry perspective.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target to have digital version ready by early September for Singapore events.
Joseph will create the webpage for the Indonesia program; Ben to provide details.
decisions.meetingTitle: RE: 关于Global Program新开印尼新兴市场项目的审批

decisions.text: Publish 'Disruptive Timing' article on English website and promote on social media.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: News Article on 'Disruptive Timing' Paper
